questions.question: If A is a set, a relation R in A is called an empty relation if:
options: R is not a subset of A × A
questions.explanation: A relation R in A is called empty relation, if no element of A is related to any element of A, i.e., R = ∅ ⊂ A × A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A relation R in a set A is called a universal relation if:
options: R is a subset of A
questions.explanation: A relation R in A is called universal relation, if each element of A is related to every element of A, i.e., R = A × A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Both the empty relation and the universal relation are sometimes called:
questions.explanation: Both the empty relation and the universal relation are sometimes called trivial relations.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A relation R in set A is reflexive if:
options: (a, a) ∈ R for every a ∈ A
(a, b) ∈ R and (b, c) ∈ R implies (a, c) ∈ R
questions.explanation: R is reflexive if (a, a) ∈ R, for every a ∈ A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A relation R in set A is symmetric if:
options: (a, b) ∈ R and (b, c) ∈ R implies (a, c) ∈ R
questions.explanation: R is symmetric if (a1, a2) ∈ R implies (a2, a1) ∈ R for all a1, a2 ∈ A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A relation R in set A is transitive if:
options: (a, b) ∈ R and (b, c) ∈ R implies (a, c) ∈ R
questions.explanation: R is transitive if (a1, a2) ∈ R and (a2, a3) ∈ R implies (a1, a3) ∈ R for all a1, a2, a3 ∈ A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A relation R in a set A is said to be an equivalence relation if R is:
questions.explanation: R is an equivalence relation if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: In the set of integers Z, the relation R = {(a, b) : 2 divides a – b} is:
questions.explanation: It is reflexive (2 divides 0), symmetric (if 2|a-b, 2|b-a), and transitive (if 2|a-b and 2|b-c, then 2|(a-b)+(b-c)).
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If R is an equivalence relation, the set of all elements related to an element 'a' is called the:
questions.explanation: The subset containing all elements related to 'a' is called the equivalence class containing 'a' and is denoted by [a].
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Equivalence classes [a] and [b] of an equivalence relation R are either:
questions.explanation: Any two equivalence classes are either identical or disjoint.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A function f: X → Y is defined to be one-one (injective) if:
options: f(x1) = f(x2) for some x1 ≠ x2
Every element in Y has a pre-image
The range is equal to the codomain
questions.explanation: A function is one-one if the images of distinct elements are distinct, which means f(x1) = f(x2) ⇒ x1 = x2.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A function f: X → Y is said to be onto (surjective) if:
options: Every element of Y is the image of some element of X
The domain is equal to the range
questions.explanation: f is onto if every element of Y is the image of some element of X under f, i.e., for every y ∈ Y, there exists x ∈ X such that f(x) = y.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A function f: X → Y is called bijective if it is:
questions.explanation: A function f: X → Y is said to be bijective if f is both one-one and onto.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A function f: X → Y is surjective if and only if:
questions.explanation: A function is onto if and only if the range of the function is equal to the codomain Y.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If f: X → Y and g: Y → Z are two functions, the composition g o f is defined as:
questions.explanation: The composition of f and g, denoted by gof, is defined as (gof)(x) = g(f(x)), for all x ∈ X.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Which mathematician first used the word 'function' in 1673 to mean a quantity varying from point to point on a curve?
questions.explanation: G. W. Leibnitz used the word 'function' in his manuscript written in 1673.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Who was the first to use the phrase 'function of x'?
questions.explanation: Leibnitz was the first to use the phrase 'function of x'.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The general adoption of symbols like f, F, φ, ψ to represent functions was made by:
questions.explanation: The general adoption of these symbols was made by Leonhard Euler in 1734.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The notation f(x) was published in 1793 by:
questions.explanation: Joseph Louis Lagrange published manuscripts in 1793 using the notation f(x).
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The set theoretic definition of function used today was developed after the work of:
questions.explanation: The set theoretic definition presently used was given after set theory was developed by Georg Cantor.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A function f: X → Y is injective if:
questions.explanation: Injective (one-one) means distinct elements have distinct images.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The set of Class XII students (A) and Class XI students (B). A relation R from A to B is a subset of:
questions.explanation: A relation R from set A to set B is a subset of the Cartesian product A × B.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A relation R in set A is reflexive if 'a' is related to ________ for all a ∈ A.
questions.explanation: Reflexivity means every element is related to itself.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If R is an equivalence relation in A, it divides A into mutually ________ subsets.
questions.explanation: An equivalence relation R in A divides A into mutually disjoint subsets Ai called partitions.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If (a, b) ∈ R and (b, a) ∈ R for all a, b, the relation is:
questions.explanation: This is the definition of symmetry.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A function that is NOT one-one is called:
questions.explanation: Otherwise (if not one-one), f is called many-one.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The identity relation in set A is denoted as:
questions.explanation: The identity relation relates every element to itself only.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If a relation is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive, it is called an:
questions.explanation: These three properties define an equivalence relation.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: For a function to be surjective, the codomain must be equal to the:
questions.explanation: A function is onto if its range matches its codomain.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Which notation did John Bernoulli use for the first time in 1718 to indicate a function of x?
questions.explanation: John Bernoulli used the notation φx in 1718.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A relation R in set A where R = A × A is called a ________ relation.
questions.explanation: The universal relation relates every element to every other element.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: In the relation 'a is brother of b', if a is brother of b, then b:
questions.explanation: The example notes if 'a' is a male student and 'b' is a female student, the relation is not symmetric.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A partition of set A by an equivalence relation R consists of ________ subsets.
questions.explanation: The equivalence classes form a partition of pairwise disjoint sets whose union is A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A one-one and onto function is also known as a:
questions.explanation: Bijection is the term for a function that is both injective and surjective.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Composition of functions g o f is defined only if the range of f is a subset of the ________ of g.
questions.explanation: To define g(f(x)), the output of f must be a valid input for g.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The phrase 'no element of A is related to any element of A' defines which relation?
questions.explanation: This is the definition of an empty relation.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The union of all equivalence classes of an equivalence relation in set A is:
questions.explanation: The union of the partitions Ai is the whole set A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Is the empty relation reflexive on a non-empty set A?
options: Only if A has one element
questions.explanation: Reflexivity requires (a, a) ∈ R for all a ∈ A. Since A is non-empty and R is empty, it fails.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: In the relation R = {(1,1), (2,2), (3,3)} on set A = {1, 2, 3}, the relation is:
questions.explanation: This is the identity relation, which is an equivalence relation.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The domain, codomain, and range are concepts associated with:
questions.explanation: These notions were introduced in Class XI for both relations and real valued functions.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If f: X → Y is injective, then f(x) = f(y) implies:
questions.explanation: This is the formal condition for a function to be one-one (injective).
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A relation where each element is related to itself and no other element is the ________ relation.
questions.explanation: The identity relation I_A on A is defined as {(a, a) : a ∈ A}.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: For the relation R = {(a, b) : a is sister of b} on a set of human beings, is it symmetric?
questions.explanation: No, because if 'a' is sister of 'b', 'b' could be the brother of 'a'.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Is the universal relation R = A × A always reflexive?
questions.explanation: Since A × A contains all possible pairs (a, b), it certainly contains all (a, a).
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: In 'Analysis Infinitorium' (1734), who established symbols for functions?
questions.explanation: Leonhard Euler made this general adoption in 1734.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If f is one-one and g is one-one, then g o f is:
questions.explanation: The composition of two injective functions is injective.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The term 'surjection' refers to which type of function?
questions.explanation: Surjective is another name for onto functions.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: What is the result of gof if f(x) = x^2 and g(x) = x+1?
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Is the relation 'a is parallel to b' on the set of lines in a plane an equivalence relation?
questions.explanation: It is reflexive (a||a), symmetric (a||b ⇒ b||a), and transitive (a||b, b||c ⇒ a||c).
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Who gave the definition of function used after set theory was developed?
questions.explanation: Subsequent to Bernoulli and Euler, Lejeunne Dirichlet gave a definition of function used for a long time.
questions.subject: Mathematics
